name: org.machanism.machai:project-layout
description: Project Layout (org.machanism.machai:project-layout) is a small Java utility library that models and detects conventional on-disk project directory layouts (sources, tests, resources, docs, modules) for use in build tooling, scanners, generators, and plugins. It provides a single abstraction (org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout) with concrete implementations for common ecosystems (Maven, Gradle, JavaScript/Node, Python, and a default fallback). The library can detect a project’s layout from its root directory (e.g., via presence of pom.xml, Gradle build files, package.json, or Python markers), then exposes root-relative folder sets such as src/main/java, src/test/java, and src/site and helper utilities for resolving/deriving paths. It includes safe recursive file/directory scanning helpers that exclude common tooling/build directories (e.g., node_modules, .git, target, build, .venv) to support indexing and processing workflows. For Maven projects it can derive layout information from pom.xml via org.machanism.machai.project.layout.PomReader and org.machanism.machai.project.layout.MavenProjectLayout. Typical usage is: choose or detect the appropriate ProjectLayout for a given project root, configure it with projectDir(java.io.File), then query getSources(), getTests(), getDocuments(), and optionally getModules() to drive compilation, code scanning, documentation generation, packaging, or analysis tasks. This artifact targets Java 8+ runtime and is intended to be embedded as a dependency in other Java tooling; it is not itself a standalone CLI but can be used inside Maven/Gradle plugins or custom command-line tools.

constructors.description: Detects a project layout implementation by inspecting a project root directory for well-known markers (Maven pom.xml, Gradle build scripts, Node package.json, Python project markers). Returns a configured org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout with its projectDir set to the provided directory.

examples.description: Detect a layout for an arbitrary repository root. The returned layout is ready to query for source/test/doc roots. This is the common entry point for scanners and tooling that must support multiple ecosystems.

constructors.description: Creates a Maven layout model. After constructing, call projectDir(java.io.File) before using getSources(), getTests(), getDocuments(), and other accessors.

examples.snippet:
java.io.File projectDir = new java.io.File("C:/work/my-maven-project");
org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout layout = new org.machanism.machai.project.layout.MavenProjectLayout().projectDir(projectDir);
examples.description: Force Maven conventions for a known Maven project. Useful when your tool already knows the project type and does not need auto-detection.

constructors.description: Creates a fallback layout when no specific build-system markers are found. It is intended to provide reasonable defaults and allow tools to still proceed when a repository is non-standard.

examples.snippet:
java.io.File projectDir = new java.io.File("C:/work/unknown-repo");
org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout layout = new org.machanism.machai.project.layout.DefaultProjectLayout().projectDir(projectDir);
examples.description: Use the default layout as a safe fallback when you want to avoid detection logic and simply scan a repository using generic conventions.

features.name: detectProjectLayout
features.description: Auto-detects the most appropriate org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout for a given repository root by checking for common build metadata files. This is the primary integration point for tools that must work across many repositories without configuration.

examples.description: Detect the project type and read the root-relative source/test/doc directories, then feed those paths into your scanner or generator.

features.name: projectDir
features.description: Configures a layout instance with the project root directory. Layout implementations are expected to be configured before any directory accessors are called.

examples.description: Set the base directory once and then query the layout. This supports fluent usage and keeps path resolution consistent.

features.name: getSources
features.description: Returns root-relative directories containing production sources for the detected/selected layout (for example, src/main/java in Maven). The returned strings are intended to be resolved against getProjectDir().

examples.description: Resolve root-relative source roots into absolute paths for scanning or compilation.

features.name: getTests
features.description: Returns root-relative directories containing test sources for the detected/selected layout (for example, src/test/java in Maven).

examples.description: Use test roots to decide whether to include tests in indexing, coverage analysis, or validation workflows.

features.name: getDocuments
features.description: Returns root-relative documentation directories for the detected/selected layout (for example, src/site for Maven site documentation).

examples.description: Index documentation roots for RAG or build documentation bundles.

features.name: findFiles
features.description: Recursively enumerates files under a directory while excluding common build/tooling folders (e.g., node_modules, .git, target, build, .venv). This is useful for repository scanning without accidentally traversing large vendor/build directories.

examples.description: Perform a safe file traversal for indexing. The exclude list helps keep scans fast and avoids transient build outputs.

features.name: scanFolder
features.description: High-level workflow for processing a project directory: detect layout, check for modules, process each module if present, otherwise process the folder as a whole. Subclasses implement processFolder(org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout) to perform domain-specific work.

examples.description: Create a tiny custom processor that prints detected layout roots. This demonstrates embedding the library inside your own tooling workflow.

customizations.name: org.machanism.machai.project.ProjectProcessor#processFolder
customizations.description: Subclass org.machanism.machai.project.ProjectProcessor and implement processFolder(org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout) to define what your tool does once a layout is detected (index sources, generate docs, analyze tests, etc.). scanFolder(java.io.File) provides module-aware traversal and calls your hook.

examples.description: Customize scanning behavior by implementing processFolder. You can combine layout-provided roots with ProjectLayout.findFiles(...) for safe traversal.

customizations.name: org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout implementations
customizations.description: Create custom layout definitions by extending org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout and implementing getSources(), getTests(), and getDocuments(). Optionally override getModules(), getProjectName(), getProjectId(), and getParentId() when those can be derived from your build metadata.

examples.description: Define an in-house convention for a monorepo and reuse the same tooling that expects a ProjectLayout instance.

examples.title: Use as a dependency (Maven) and detect a repository layout
examples.goal: Add the library to your tool and automatically detect whether a repository is Maven/Gradle/Node/Python, then list its key roots.

steps.instruction: Add the dependency to your tool project (pom.xml).
Write code that detects the layout and prints source/test/doc roots.
Resolve each root-relative path to an absolute java.io.File before touching the filesystem.

examples.notes: This scenario is typical for repository scanners and code generators that need to support multiple ecosystems without per-repo configuration.

examples.title: Build a small CLI-like tool (using exec:java) that lists all files excluding build directories
examples.goal: Demonstrate a ready-to-run workflow from Maven that invokes code using ProjectLayout.findFiles(...) while automatically skipping node_modules/target/build/etc.

steps.instruction: Add dependencies and the Exec Maven Plugin to your tool project.
Create a main class that lists files under a provided directory.
Run it using Maven (this is effectively a simple CLI invocation).

examples.notes: This example demonstrates an end-to-end runnable component pattern (a tiny CLI) that uses the library. The exclusion rules in ProjectLayout help keep the output stable and avoid scanning build artifacts.
